task: Complete Stage 3 of stop-hook refactor: tag auto-suggested goals at session start and expire them at checkpoint end
discussion_summary: Session completed all three stages of the stop-hook checkpoint refactor. Stage 1 ensured the stop hook runs first as an authoritative checkpoint. Stage 2 added a timestamp gate to skip stale checkpoint_pending.json files. Stage 3 implemented goal lifecycle management: auto-suggested goals are now tagged when inferred at session start, and expired (removed) at checkpoint end after completed goals are processed. All changes verified clean in venv Python and pushed to main. The Python 3.10+ union syntax error in reader.py is pre-existing and unrelated to this session's hook changes.

accomplishments.what: Stage 1: Stop hook authoritative checkpoint — always runs first before any other checkpoint logic
Stage 2: Timestamp gate on checkpoint_pending.json — skip processing if file is stale (older than 5 min)
Stage 3: Auto-suggested goal expiry — added expire_auto_suggested_goals() function to goals.py
Stage 3: Tag goals at session start — modified session_start.py to mark inferred goals with auto_suggested=True
Stage 3: Call expiry at checkpoint end — integrated expire_auto_suggested_goals() into checkpoint.py after completed goals processed
All changes verified clean and pushed to origin/main

next_actions.action: Commit and push the two uncommitted tracking files: askr_state/implementation_state.md and roadmap.md. These contain session activity logs and phase roadmap updates that should be in version control.
next_actions.why: Uncommitted files remain in working tree; these are documentation that tracks progress and should be persisted

next_actions.action: Run full integration test: start a session, auto-suggest goals, end session, verify checkpoint_pending.json is created, then start a new session and confirm auto-suggested goals from previous session are expired and not present
next_actions.why: Stage 3 implementation is complete but end-to-end lifecycle has not been tested in a real session flow

next_actions.action: Verify context checkpoint cards display correct 'turns remaining' in staging environment — check report_image.py turns-until-auto-compact calculation
next_actions.why: This is an open goal from the session that was not yet addressed; it is a prerequisite for stress testing

next_actions.why: Second open goal; depends on completion of action 3

next_actions.action: Begin Phase 3.11 JSON Handover Schema implementation (next roadmap item) — design and implement the handover document structure for autonomous agent continuity
next_actions.why: Roadmap shows this as the next phase after stop-hook refactor completion

decisions.decision: Auto-suggested goals are tagged at inference time (session_start.py) rather than at goal creation time
decisions.reason: Allows distinction between user-created goals and system-inferred goals; enables selective expiry without affecting user intent

decisions.decision: Expiry happens at checkpoint end, after completed goals are processed, not at session start
decisions.reason: Ensures completed auto-suggested goals are recorded in checkpoint before removal; prevents loss of completion signal

decisions.decision: Timestamp gate uses 5-minute staleness threshold on checkpoint_pending.json
decisions.reason: Balances avoiding stale checkpoints while allowing legitimate multi-session workflows with brief pauses

failed_approaches.approach: Testing with system Python 3.9 (python3 -c import test)
failed_approaches.reason: Union type syntax (|) in reader.py requires Python 3.10+; venv has correct version but system Python does not. Pre-existing issue, not caused by this session.

relational_files.why: Core goal state management; expire_auto_suggested_goals() function added here and called from checkpoint.py and session_start.py
Calls add_goal() with auto_suggested=True tag when inferring goals at session start
Calls expire_auto_suggested_goals() at checkpoint end after completed goals are processed
